Docker registry trust is partial — login: Docker registry trust is partial — login, CA install, and insecure/mirror config (daemon.json) work; there is no per-registry search-order (Docker lacks it) and system-wide writes may need elevation. Podman manages registries natively.

The SSH connection tunnels the {{engine}: The SSH connection tunnels the {{engine}} API only, not published ports. `-p {{hostPort}}:{{containerPort}}` bound `0.0.0.0:{{hostPort}}` on the remote host, where `{{container}}` is listening — so `{{from}}` here reaches nothing. The forward reaches the container fine,: The forward reaches the container fine, but the service is bound to `127.0.0.1:{{containerPort}}` — so it rejects the connection arriving on the container's network interface. Bind the service to `0.0.0.0` (all interfaces).
The log file could not be opened — check: The log file could not be opened — check its permissions.

Two axes decide each cell. Engine dialec: Two axes decide each cell. Engine dialect — Podman speaks libpod (pods, secrets, kube, native Compose); Docker speaks the docker API (Swarm). Transport — machine lifecycle only works on a native Podman, never over SSH. The clock marks capabilities on the roadmap the app doesn't detect yet — never "broken," just not wired.

You can reuse “{{name}}” instead of crea: You can reuse “{{name}}” instead of creating a new one — the wizard will pick it up automatically.

Your engine is set up with shared folder: Your engine is set up with shared folders and permissions that just work.
Your saved AI permissions could not be r: Your saved AI permissions could not be read, so the assistant is asking before every command. Fix or reset the file below.
`{{dev}}`{{app}} installed full-tunnel r: `{{dev}}`{{app}} installed full-tunnel routes {{routes}}, which swallow traffic leaving your container's subnet — so container egress is dropped while the VPN is up. Add a split-tunnel exclusion, or move the network onto a range the VPN doesn't claim.
`{{from}}` reaches `{{container}}:{{cont: `{{from}}` reaches `{{container}}:{{containerPort}}`; the service is listening on `0.0.0.0:{{containerPort}}` and answered. Nothing to fix.
`{{service}}` and `{{from}}` aren't on a: `{{service}}` and `{{from}}` aren't on a shared network
`{{service}}` resolves on a network shar: `{{service}}` resolves on a network shared with `{{from}}` and answered on port {{containerPort}}. Nothing to fix.
cosign could not verify this image: cosign could not verify this image
cosign is signed in to {{registry}} but: cosign is signed in to {{registry}} but couldn't read this image — it may be private (no access) or not published to that registry (e.g. a local build).
docker command works on Windows but not: docker command works on Windows but not inside the WSL distro
name: name
permission denied while trying to connec: permission denied while trying to connect to the Docker daemon socket /var/run/docker.sock
testcontainers isn't detected yet — it r: testcontainers isn't detected yet — it runs against any Docker-API socket (Podman exposes a Docker-compatible one too).
{{engine}}'s DNS only resolves container: {{engine}}'s DNS only resolves container names within the same network. `{{from}}` is on `{{fromNetworks}}`; `{{service}}` is only on `{{targetNetworks}}` — no overlap, so the name can't resolve. Attach one to the other's network.
{{registry}} requires sign-in before thi: {{registry}} requires sign-in before this image's manifest and signature can be read. Sign in and verification resumes automatically.
